title: China close to cracking code of tech that controls the world - all about EUV machine
content: China has reportedly developed a functional prototype of an EUV lithography system. This development challenges Western assumptions about China's chip manufacturing capabilities. The system utilizes a mix of indigenous breakthroughs and shadow procurement networks. Experts suggest mass production could be achieved by 2030. This marks a significant shift in the global semiconductor landscape.

title: The Bengaluru Mercedes incident is just the tip of India's massive parking nightmare
content: India faces a significant civic problem with widespread illegal vehicle parking.  Rapid vehicle ownership growth has outpaced parking infrastructure development across cities.  Pedestrians are often forced off footpaths due to encroaching vehicles and other obstructions.  Enforcement of parking rules remains inconsistent, encouraging repeat violations by drivers.  Addressing this requires better infrastructure, consistent enforcement, and public cooperation.

title: CSK part ways with Stephen Fleming; Hemang Badani in fray to join set-up
content: Chennai Super Kings (CSK) have ended Stephen Fleming's stint as head coach as the franchise reviews its cricket operations after two disappointing IPL seasons. Hemang Badani is in contention to join the set-up, while CSK's leadership is also evaluating trades, the coaching staff and the captaincy ahead of IPL 2027.

title: SC stays Madras HC order directing total ban on cow slaughter during Bakrid
content: A vacation bench comprising Justice GR Swaminathan and Justice V Lakshminarayanan passed the order while hearing a public interest litigation (PIL) seeking to prevent the slaughter of cows in places other than designated slaughterhouses. "The question that calls for consideration in this PIL is whether cows and calves can be sacrificed in places not designated as slaughterhouses on the occasion of Bakrid?" the bench observed.

title: How Apple's lawsuit could end Sam Altman's AI device dreams
content: Apple has sued OpenAI, alleging theft of trade secrets and intellectual property. The lawsuit targets OpenAI's nascent artificial intelligence hardware development program. AI commentator Shruti Mishra suggests this legal action could significantly delay OpenAI's hardware plans. Apple seeks a preliminary injunction to halt OpenAI's hardware advancements during litigation. This legal challenge may impact OpenAI's planned AI hardware device launch.

title: 'Terminator 6': Yuvraj Singh gives Vaibhav Sooryavanshi a new nickname
content: Yuvraj Singh has given Vaibhav Sooryavanshi the nickname "Terminator 6". He sees the young player as a serious talent with a bright future. Singh previously mentored Abhishek Sharma and wishes to guide Sooryavanshi too. Both players attended the Wimbledon men's singles final recently. Sooryavanshi expressed his excitement about learning from his idol Yuvraj.

title: 'Citizenship process must be fair': SC relief for 27 declared foreigners in Assam
content: The Supreme Court has overturned the Gauhati High Court's rulings on foreigner status, with Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta calling for a re-examination of cases by tribunals. They highlighted that citizenship matters hold significant constitutional weight and must be approached with procedural fairness, stressing the state's duty to safeguard against misuse of citizenship assertions.

title: Ousted Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal says Microsoft's Satya Nadella inspired him to become a founder
content: Parag Agrawal found inspiration in Satya Nadella's leadership for Twitter's transformation. He viewed Nadella as a re-founder who completely changed a business and culture. Agrawal planned radical internal projects like Project Saturn and Birdwatch for Twitter. These initiatives aimed to significantly alter user experience and content moderation. His tenure ended abruptly, preventing the full realization of these ambitious plans.

title: Gold price prediction: Any end to gold fall in sight? Check outlook for this week
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are trading with a bearish bias as geopolitical events weigh on investor sentiment, says Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.

title: Lindsey Graham’s final hours: Trump call, Ukraine trip and cardiac emergency
content: Veteran South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham has died at the age of 71 after what his office described as a "brief and sudden illness." Graham passed away on Saturday night, just a day after returning from an official trip to Ukraine, where he had met Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky and worked on discussions related to fresh sanctions against Russia.

title: Customer left lottery tickets at Circle K, one later won $12.8M
content: A legal battle has emerged over the rightful ownership of a winning lottery ticket worth $12.8 million, with a Circle K store clerk maintaining that he simply followed company policy when he purchased a set of abandoned lottery tickets. According to reports, Circle K filed a complaint in February against store clerk Robert Gawlitza, alleging that he attempted to claim the jackpot from a winning Arizona Lottery ticket that had been left behind by a customer at one of its stores on November 24, 2025.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the nonprofit's history. Active Minds focuses on youth mental health awareness and reducing stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support young leaders. This donation occurs amid growing concerns about youth mental health.

title: 'We're going to put Messi to bed': Cole's warning before England vs Argentina clash
content: England and Argentina will meet in a 2026 World Cup semifinal match. Former England player Joe Cole believes his nation can stop Lionel Messi. Both teams have battled hard to reach this crucial semifinal stage. Argentina are the defending champions and seek consecutive World Cup titles. This highly anticipated match will occur at Atlanta Stadium.

title: WhatsApp, SMS or email: Can your landlord legally evict you through a text?
content: To validly terminate a lease, it's essential to use the correct legal format. Informal electronic communications like SMS or WhatsApp typically lack the legal enforceability needed. However, Indian courts could accept emails if their authenticity is established. Casual messages do not meet the legal requirement for written and signed termination notices. It's crucial for landlords to adhere to these legal protocols to prevent disputes with tenants.

title: Five students who just made India world number 1 in Physics
content: Five Indian students—Kanishk Jain, Riddhesh Anant Bendale, Rishit Garg, Shresth Suraiya and Svarit Joshi—created history by winning gold medals at the 56th International Physics Olympiad 2026 in Colombia. Their achievement helped India rank joint World No. 1 alongside leading science nations. The feat highlights the strength of India’s Olympiad training ecosystem and showcases the advanced problem-solving, theoretical knowledge and experimental skills required to succeed at one of the world’s toughest physics competitions.

title: China cries foul after 14 nations reject Beijing's South China Sea claims
content: Accusing Japan of interfering in regional affairs and undermining peace and stability, China insisted that its sovereignty over the disputed waters has "never changed" despite the 2016 tribunal ruling. The diplomatic row erupted after Japan, along with the United States, Australia, the United Kingdom, the Philippines and nine other countries, marked the 10th anniversary of the Permanent Court of Arbitration's ruling by reiterating that China's expansive maritime claims have "no legal basis" under international law.

title: Cody Rhodes vs Seth Rollins net worth: Who earns more in WWE?
content: Two of WWE’s biggest stars have emerged in Seth Rollins and Cody Rhodes, whose wrestling accomplishments, title wins and commercial appeal have driven interest around the world. In this comparison, the article takes a look at their projected 2026 net worth, WWE salaries, endorsements, investments, business ventures, lavish lifestyles and earning potential in the future. The article points out that both superstars made their money in different ways.

title: Fact check: Did Erling Haaland unfollow Alexander Sorloth on Instagram?
content: The claim that Erling Haaland unfollowed Alexander Sorloth on Instagram after Norway’s World Cup exit is false. The rumour came from a parody social media account after Sorloth chose not to pass to Haaland during a key attack against England. Haaland still follows his teammate on Instagram, and there is no evidence of any dispute between the two players.

title: First time in 36 years! Argentina, France, Spain, England set up historic semifinals
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 semifinals showcase the top four ranked teams, a rare occurrence. Argentina will face England in a historic rivalry match after their quarterfinal wins. France and Spain meet in the other semifinal, continuing their competitive history. Both Messi and Mbappé are leading the Golden Boot race with eight goals. The final is scheduled for July 20 at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey.

title: Dhar's Saraswati is Gayatri: Science ends 900-year mix-up
content: A 12th-century sculpture previously identified as Saraswati has been correctly identified as Gayatri. Digital documentation and 3D mapping revealed iconographic clues missed for centuries. The absence of a veena, a common Saraswati attribute, proved crucial in this re-identification. This rare image of Gayatri, embodying Vedic wisdom, was unearthed in Dhar. The rediscovered icon now represents Madhya Pradesh's digital heritage mission.

title: BJP sends legal notice to CM Omar Abdullah over 'Rs 30 crore offer' claim
content: The Bharatiya Janata Party has sent a legal notice to Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah. This action follows allegations made by Abdullah regarding a BJP representative's bribe offer. The notice describes the chief minister's claims as false and defamatory to the party's reputation. It demands an unconditional public apology within seven days from Abdullah. Failure to comply will result in civil and criminal proceedings seeking substantial damages.

title: Badrinath donation theft case: Temple employee Pramod Nautiyal arrested
content: The action comes a day after the Uttarakhand government directed the chief executive officer of the Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee (BKTC) to initiate action against officials and employees found responsible for alleged financial irregularities, following an inquiry into payments made for the accommodation, food and other expenses of VIP guests at the Kedarnath establishment.

title: 'This is who...': Gardner's estranged wife names Aus teammate in cheating allegation
content: Australian cricket vice-captain Ashleigh Gardner faces controversy after her estranged wife made infidelity claims. Monica Wright publicly accused Gardner of cheating and named teammate Georgia Voll. The marriage reportedly deteriorated quickly after their wedding in April 2025. Wright removed wedding photos from her social media. Neither Gardner nor Voll has publicly commented on the allegations.

title: Rohit Sharma undertakes match-simulation in nets ahead of ODI series opener
content: Rohit Sharma put in an extensive match-simulation batting session during India's training at Edgbaston ahead of the first ODI against England, while Virat Kohli sharpened his game against both pace and spin before sharing valuable insights with young pacer Gurnoor Brar as preparations gathered momentum.

title: 'Khela Hobey Divas' now 'Ayushman Divas': BJP's tit-for-tat answer to Didi
content: Mamata Banerjee's government had declared August 16 as Khela Hobey Divas in 2021 following the TMC's assembly election victory. The day was officially promoted as a sports initiative aimed at encouraging young people through football tournaments and the distribution of sports equipment. However, the slogan was an apparent dig as Didi had led her last term campaign with "Khela Hobey" slogan.

title: CM Chandrababu Naidu visits Pawan Kalyan after surgery, says he is recovering well
content: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu visited Pawan Kalyan in Mumbai. The Deputy Chief Minister underwent successful surgery on his right shoulder. Doctors confirmed the operation was completed successfully and recovery is progressing well. Pawan Kalyan has been advised three weeks of physiotherapy for rehabilitation. He is reportedly eager to resume his official responsibilities soon.

title: 'Beast Mode': Why China's 'fully loaded' J-16 fighter is setting off alarm bells
content: The J-16 fighter jet of China now features an impressive weapons loadout, enhancing its operational prowess. This new configuration allows it to function as a formidable missile platform in aerial engagements. Meanwhile, the People's Liberation Army Air Force is swiftly advancing its combat aircraft modernization efforts, alongside upgrades to its long-range support capabilities with improved transport and refueling assets, underscoring a strategic shift towards military dominance.

title: 'We heard an explosion, then flames spread': Survivors recount Bangkok pub fire; 27 killed
content: In a horrific incident, a raging fire swept through a crowded pub in Bangkok, resulting in the deaths of 27 individuals. Panic ensued as smoke enveloped the venue, with numerous victims cornered at the rear due to obstructed emergency exits. Eyewitnesses shared their desperate attempts to escape the chaos as the inferno blazed shortly before midnight.

title: This US inmate wants pasta in Delhi's Tihar Jail. But what does a prisoner's meal look like?
content: American Matthew VanDyke, facing terror charges, seeks permission to cook his own meals. He claims Tihar Jail's food is unsuitable and has been on a hunger strike. VanDyke, a former war correspondent, was arrested by the NIA for alleged drone training activities. Tihar Jail follows a fixed diet plan for its over 20,000 inmates. A Delhi court will hear VanDyke's plea for a special diet on July 21.

title: Rupee slips 39 paise against US dollar as oil prices soar again
content: The Indian rupee opened lower against the US dollar on Monday. Soaring crude oil prices and Middle East tensions dampened market sentiment. Brent crude prices surged after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed. Foreign investors continued to support Indian equities despite market volatility. India's foreign exchange reserves increased by over seven billion dollars.

title: 'Jab main unse pehli baar mila ... ': Sooryavanshi opens up on Yuvraj's influence
content: Teenage cricketer Vaibhav Sooryavanshi attended the Wimbledon men's singles final. He watched Jannik Sinner win his second Wimbledon title on Sunday. Sooryavanshi was accompanied by former India all-rounder Yuvraj Singh. Abhishek Sharma also joined them, recalling his own admiration for Yuvraj. The young player expressed gratitude for Yuvraj's guidance and Sharma's assistance.

title: 'Smoke was everywhere': How Bangkok's pub inferno killed 27 - 10 points
content: Tragedy struck at Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub, where a fierce fire resulted in the deaths of twenty-seven people. Victims found themselves ensnared in the pub's bathrooms as they tried to escape. Smoke filled the venue quickly, contributing to the catastrophe. Emergency responders faced significant hurdles when attempting to rescue those inside. Authorities have launched an investigation to uncover what led to this heartbreaking event.

title: Woman can't claim maintenance from second man without divorcing first husband: HC
content: The Allahabad high court dismissed a woman's maintenance claim against Santosh Kumar. She had begun living with Santosh without divorcing her first husband, Sharda Prasad. The court noted her own admission that no Hindu marriage rites were performed. However, maintenance for their daughter was upheld based on a DNA report. Santosh Kumar's petition was partly allowed by the court's decision.

title: Entry to 136-yr-old Kolkata airport mosque stopped indefinitely
content: Kolkata airport authorities have indefinitely stopped entry to the Bankra mosque. Security concerns were raised by the Bureau of Civil Aviation Security regarding Aadhaar card access. Previous repairs to the approach road are now complete for devotees. Airport officials await new instructions on a potential pass system. Local leaders are seeking clarity and discussing community action.

title: Nifty Realty Index: How NSE measures performance of real estate sector
content: The Nifty Realty Index tracks India's listed real estate companies' performance. It comprises ten firms selected by free-float market capitalization and rebalanced semi-annually. DLF, Phoenix Mills, and Lodha Developers hold the largest weights within the index. Companies must meet specific criteria, including trading volume and market capitalization for inclusion. This index serves as a benchmark and basis for financial products, reflecting sector sentiment.

title: MTET 2026 hall ticket released: Teacher eligibility examination on July 24
content: The Directorate of Educational Research and Training, Meghalaya, has released the MTET 2026 admit card for candidates appearing in the teacher eligibility examination on July 24. The hall ticket can be downloaded using the registration number and password through the official portal. The examination will be conducted in offline mode for Paper I and Paper II. Candidates facing download issues may contact DERT or visit their district DIET centre for assistance with obtaining the admit card.

title: Kospi dream rally fumbles: World's best-performing stock plunges 7%
content: South Korea's Kospi index experienced a significant 7% decline on Monday. Rising Middle East tensions and oil price spikes rattled global investors worldwide. Technology stocks, including SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics, led the sharp fall. The market correction intensified concerns over AI-linked semiconductor company valuations. Despite the recent drop, the Kospi remains a top global performer this year.

title: 'Will our home still be standing tomorrow?' The Northeast relives its annual flood heartbreak
content: Northeast India faces severe monsoon floods and landslides, affecting nearly 50,000 people in Assam. Rising waters have submerged villages and damaged agricultural land, impacting livelihoods. Across the region, homes and infrastructure have suffered extensive damage from heavy rainfall. Thousands have been displaced and are seeking refuge in the relief camps.

title: 'Jaldi jaldi me jo mila ... ': Sooryavanshi reveals Abhishek arranged his Wimbledon outfit
content: Teenage Indian cricketer Vaibhav Sooryavanshi attended the Wimbledon men's singles final. He watched Jannik Sinner win his second Wimbledon title against Alexander Zverev. Sooryavanshi, who recently debuted for India, was dressed formally for the occasion. He admitted his outfit was arranged quickly in the last minute. Several other Indian cricketers were also present at the prestigious event.

title: Who are Jannik Sinner’s parents? Meet dad Johann and mom Siglinde
content: Jannik Sinner, the world No. 1 tennis player and four-time Grand Slam champion, credits much of his drive to his parents, Johann and Siglinde Sinner. The couple worked for years at a ski lodge in the Italian Dolomites before the family established their own guest house. Johann occasionally joins Sinner on tour as a personal chef, while Siglinde is known to struggle watching her son's matches live due to nerves. Both were present at Wimbledon 2025 when Sinner defended his title.

title: NHL trade rumours: Utah Mammoth, Sharks eye $59M Winnipeg Jets goalie
content: The Winnipeg Jets’ star goaltender Connor Hellebuyck has been linked to the Buffalo Sabres heavily in the last few weeks before the deal reportedly fell through, a few days ago. While he has since been speculated to join a number of rival teams, nothing has been confirmed yet. Amid this, the Utah Mammoth and the San Jose Sharks have emerged as top landing spots.

title: CAG flags Kochi Corporation’s idling vehicle fleet bleeding public money
content: CAG reports finds that out of the Corporation’s 143 vehicles, 72 had to be auctioned off as unserviceable, while another 34 were off the roads for want of fitness or maintenance

title: SC issues notice in petitions in the Ram temple donations' embezzlement case | LIVE
content: A petition filed by RJD MP Sudhakar Singh to provide and publish complete financial details of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, including foreign contributions, is listed along with two other petitions

title: Efforts on to fly home remains of three Andhra tourists killed in Vietnam boat accident
content: Embassy of India in Vietnam has finalised the authorised agency that will facilitate the repatriation of the mortal remains of the deceased to India

title: Supreme Court stays Madras HC order directing ban on cow slaughter in Tamil Nadu
content: The High Court had ordered a blanket ban on the slaughter of cows and calves anywhere in the state on May 27

title: Naidu to launch ₹152.95-crore Cotton barrage gates renovation on July 13
content: The ₹152.95-crore renovation project is expected to be completed by July 2028; a total of 58 gates have been replaced with ₹31.77 crore during the 2014-15 financial year

title: Pamban Bridge inauguration highlights: PM Modi inaugurates new rail bridge in Rameswaram
content: Inaugurating the new Pamban bridge in Rameswaram, PM Modi says it is the country’s first vertical lift railway sea bridge, adding efforts were on to take Tamil language, heritage to all corners of the world

title: Madras HC to take up on July 13 MRK Panneerselvam’s plea to be discharged from 2011 disproportionate assets case
content: This is the second discharge plea; the first plea was allowed by a magistrate in 2016 but reversed by the High Court in 2025

title: EPFO Amnesty Scheme 2026: What is it and who is eligible?
content: The EPFO has launched the Amnesty Scheme 2026, aimed at providing a six-month window for establishments managing Provident Fund (PF) Trusts under the Income Tax Act of 1961 to regularise their compliance status.

title: Supreme Court rejects claims of over 360 West Bengal madrasas for State salary
content: The case came up before the Supreme Court with questions including whether the madrasas in which the petitioners were appointed were validly recognised

title: Chenthamara found guilty in Nenmara double murder in Kerala; sentencing on July 15
content: Chenthamara, the lone accused, appeared remorseless as the verdict was delivered. Asked if he had anything to say, he told the court to ‘hang’ him

title: Sahitya Akademi award-winning Tamil writer Poomani passes away
content: Poomani received the Sahitya Akademi Award in 2014 for his monumental novel Agnaadi, an epic narrative set against the backdrop of the Sivakasi (1899) and Kazhugumalai (1895) communal riots; Chief Minister Vijay announces State honours

title: Kashmir’s July 13 Martyrs’ Day: History and controversy
content: Here’s how the events of July 13, 1931, a mass shooting in Srinagar, became Kashmir’s Martyrs’ Day and changed the Valley’s political history

title: Supreme Court agrees to list all petitions challenging HC decision in Bhojshala case
content: The Hindu community considers Bhojshala a temple dedicated to Goddess Saraswati, while the Muslim side calls the 11th-century monument the Kamal Maula Mosque

title: Kolkata-based Berry Alloys to invest ₹1,200 crore in steel, metals complex in Vizianagaram
content: The State government cleared the Kolkata firm’s integrated plant at the Bobbili Growth Centre, to produce manganese sinter, DRI and carbon paste, and allotted 94 acres at a concessional rate

title: Amarnath pilgrims among 18 injured in multi-vehicle collision in Jammu-Kashmir's Ramban
content: The accident occurred outside the Chanderkote Langar point at around 7.20 a.m. after one of the buses allegedly suffered a brake failure, leading to a chain collision involving one more bus and a car said officials

title: Controversial Kerala YouTuber Thoppi’s channel shut down over malicious content
content: Cyber police had earlier booked Mohammad Nihad for allegedly posting obscene visuals through streaming platforms

title: Retrenchments in Kerala: Labour codes and its rules | Explained
content: Chapter XI of the Code provides that the appropriate Government shall set up a worker re-skilling fund, and the employer must contribute to the fund an amount equal to 15 days’ wages last drawn by the worker immediately before the retrenchment

title: Karnataka Governor suspends KPSC chairperson Shivashankarappa S Sahukar over 'illegal selection' of daughters in government service
content: The Governor recommended to the President that the matter be referred to the Supreme Court under Article 317(1) of the Constitution for an inquiry into the allegations

title: BJP SC Morcha seeks UAPA action against Prakash Raj, Jada Sravan over remarks on Hindu beliefs
content: It urges State government to take legal action against certain individuals over their alleged remarks on Hindu beliefs

title: ‘Khaunga, khane doonga aur khilaunga’: Congress slams PM Modi’s anti-graft pitch
content: The explosive growth of the Modani empire revealed the true nature of the Modi regime, as was proved by the 100 questions asked of the Prime Minister in the Congress’ ‘Hum Adani Ke Hain Kaun’ series, said Jairam Ramesh

title: BJP issues ₹100 crore defamation notice to Omar Abdullah over ‘MLA poaching’ charges
content: Calling the allegations “false, baseless and defamatory”, the BJP has demanded a written retraction and an unconditional public apology within seven days, and warned of civil and criminal proceedings if the demands are not met

title: Pawan Kalyan undergoes shoulder surgery in Mumbai
content: Surgery for the left shoulder, which also has its rotator cuff injured, will be performed after two months, according to a press release

title: Efforts on to repatriate remains of three Andhra tourists killed in Vietnam boat accident
content: The remains of Nallapeta Adiseshaiah Ravi Teja, Gelli Jaya Lakshmi and Mudium Sreedhar are to be flown home; the injured Gelli Kishore remains critical and may be moved to Ho Chi Minh City

title: ‘We tried CPR, but it was too late,’ say Vietnam boat tragedy witnesses on return to Hyderabad
content: Several Indian tourists who were stranded in Vietnam after the tragedy landed in Hyderabad on the night of July 12

title: We need encouragement, recognition more than money, says Kalyana Karnataka-based folk artist
content: Rajyotsava award winner Hanifa M. Shaikh shares her journey at a programme organised by Kannada and Culture Department and Rangayana

title: YSRCP using disgruntled farmers as pawns to stall Amaravati, alleges Ananda Babu
content: The opposition party is trying to stall development of the capital city project by using a small number of disgruntled farmers as pawns in its political game, alleges Nakka Ananda Babu

title: Kerala observes State mourning following demise of Qatar’s former ruler Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani
content: The government, through an order, directed that there shall be no official entertainment or celebratory State functions during the period of mourning

title: Bengal to observe 'Ayushman Divas' on Aug 16, replacing TMC's 'Khela Hobey Divas': CM
content: "The previous TMC government observed it as 'Khela Hobey Divas'. Our government has decided that the day will be observed as Ayushman Divas. The details of our programme will soon follow," says West Bengal CM

title: Three militants arrested in Manipur
content: An insurgent belonging to the banned United Peoples' Party of Kangleipak (UPPK) outfit was apprehended in Porompat area in Imphal East district on July 13

title: Suvendu Adhikari backs curbs on Kolkata airport mosque entry, cites security concerns
content: The location of the Kolkata international airport is critical since both China and Bangladesh are close by; it cannot keep its gates open for outsiders, said Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari

title: Road rage | Politics of renaming a street in Bengal
content: Soon after the BJP government renamed Kolkata’s Suhrawardy Avenue as Gopal Mukherjee Road, there was public outcry — and historical evidence — that the street was originally named not after the man whom the government blames for killings, but rather the first Muslim V-C of Calcutta University. Here’s why the renaming of the street marks a crucial moment in Bengal politics

title: ISRO successfully carries out tests of Gaganyaan crew module systems
content: One of the tests involved separation of the ‘umbilical mechanism’ that serves as a link between the crew module, where astronauts live, and the service module that provides propulsion, says the agency

title: BJP, RSS, VHP have hijacked Lord Ram, says former MP V. S. Ugrappa
content: He told reporters in Belagavi that none of the 11 members of the temple trust, including a retired government officer, were able to prevent the alleged theft

title: Central Vista to be known as ‘Kartavya Bhawan Area’: HUA Minister Manohar Lal
content: Until now, we have been repeatedly referring to it as ‘Central Vista’; from now on, please call it the ‘Kartavya Bhawan Complex’ or ‘Kartavya Bhawan Area’, says Union Housing and Urban Affairs Minister Manohar Lal

title: CAG flags ₹3,541 crore excess spend, budgetary lapses in Maharashtra Govt’s Ladki Bahin scheme
content: The CAG said audit scrutiny revealed that ₹15,586 crore drawn between January and March 2025 was transferred to Virtual Personal Deposit Accounts (VPDAs)

title: Supreme Court to hear plea against sky-high airfares today
content: The petition said the government’s answer that “new rules are being framed, trust the process”  was just not enough

title: SIR anomalies may cross one crore in Telangana: CEO
content: In an exclusive interview with The Hindu, Chief Electoral Officer C. Sudharshan Reddy provides more insights into the ongoing SIR process

title: Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary inaugurates projects worth ₹1,047 crore
content: Mr. Choudhary said that Bihar’s economy was being significantly strengthened by promoting women’s economic empowerment and entrepreneurship.

title: West Bengal government seeks support of DMSC for cervical cancer vaccination for sex workers in Sonagachi
content: Durbar Mahila Samanwaya Committee, a collective for working on rights and recognition of sex workers, celebrates 31 years

title: Prospects of kharif cultivation turns bleak in Telangana with monsoon failure
content: Over 63% of mandals in 32 rural districts record deficient to large deficient rainfall; water levels in reservoirs remains gloomy

title: Hyderabad SI suspended after letting six-year-old granddaughter drive, claims he was preparing her for Guinness World Record
content: Video shows the child behind the wheel on the busy Gandhamguda–Bairagiguda road as traffic piles up behind the car; the vehicle already had six pending traffic challans worth ₹3,800

title: Mehbooba placed under ‘house arrest’ ahead of Martyrs’ Day; PDP raises rights concerns
content: Says democratic society cannot flourish where people continue to live under an environment of constant apprehension

title: Singer Janaki cremated with state honours in Mysuru
content: Janaki’s granddaughter Apsara Vydyula performed the last rites as per Andhra Brahmin traditions in the presence of her close relatives, people’s representatives, friends, and a large number of admirers and the general public

title: Doctor who flagged equipment shortage lauds Kerala government’s move to end floor treatment
content: Haris Chirakkal, Head of Urology department at Government Medical College Hospital, Thiruvananthapuram, had hit the headlines after flagging shortages of essential surgical equipment and delays in surgeries during the previous LDF government’s tenure

title: AAP launches signature drive to press for action against ‘dacoity’ at Ram Temple
content: Kejriwal urges devotees across the country to organise Hanuman Chalisa recitals and collect signatures

title: Ernakulam to get government-run geriatric centre
content: The district panchayat has initiated steps to establish a geriatric centre which will include an inpatient physiotherapy centre and a dialysis centre

title: Palakkad-Kozhikode highway construction to get under way soon
content: Six-lane highway under the Union government’s Bharatmala project is back on track, with over 95% of land acquisition completed

title: Foreign Investors Pump Over $1 Billion Into Indian Stock Market In A Week
content: The return of foreign investors may help sustain recovery in the NSE Nifty 50 index, according to Goldman Sachs strategists.

title: Entry To Kolkata Airport Mosque Stopped, Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari Backs Move
content: The 136-year-old Gauripur Jama Masjid, also known as the 'Bankra Masjid', is located within Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International airport.

title: Odisha Man Dies After Wife Hits Him On Head With Mobile During Fight
content: Reports suggest that an argument broke out between the husband and wife at their residence on Saturday. During the altercation, the wife allegedly hit her husband on the head with a mobile phone

title: Europe Records Over 10,000 Excess Deaths Amid June Heatwave: Report
content: Scientists have said the late-June heatwave would have been "virtually impossible" without human-caused climate change, which is making heatwaves more frequent and intense.

title: From Trump Critic To Trusted Ally: The Political Journey Of Lindsey Graham
content: Graham, aged 71, died Saturday night after what a preliminary report from the Washington, DC, medical examiner said was a tear in his aorta.

title: Vijay Government Secures Supreme Court Stay Against Cow Slaughter Ban Order
content: Tamil Nadu government, led by Chief Minister Vijay, secures a Supreme Court stay against the Madras High Court's cow slaughter ban order.

title: 2,700 Killed By Heatwaves Across England Between May And June: Study
content: The UK and most of Europe experienced two unprecedented heatwaves in May and June, with monthly records set at 35.1 degrees Celsius and 37.7 degrees Celsius, respectively, in England.

title: Who Will Replace Lindsey Graham? Battle Begins For Senate Seat
content: After the death of Lindsey Graham, Governor McMaster must choose a temporary replacement who can serve until the voters choose a new Republican nominee for the general election in January.

title: Own More Than Four Credit Cards? New Data Reveals A Hidden Risk
content: Maintaining a balanced credit portfolio and avoiding excessive dependence on revolving credit are key factors that preserve a healthy credit profile.

title: India's Theatre Command Plan Nears Approval After 20 Years Of Talk
content: If the proposal clears the Cabinet Committee on Security, India would join a small club of major military powers that already operate through integrated joint commands rather than separate service structures.

title: Trump, Meloni, Macron And More: List Of World Leaders On Iran's Kill List
content: The Hamshahri newspaper, which is published by authorities in the Iranian capital, released an infographic online late Saturday featuring photos of 13 foreign leaders alongside Mojtaba's statement.

title: Iran Claims US Military's Drone Fleet In Bahrain Destroyed In Fresh Strikes
content: The US military began launching more strikes against Iran on Sunday, with US Central Command (Centcom) saying it hit "dozens of targets at multiple locations with precision munitions to degrade Iran's ability to continue attacking international shipping flowing through the Strait of Hormuz".

title: Gwalior Chartered Accountant Loses Rs 21 Crore In Fake Investment Scam
content: A 70-year-old Gwalior chartered accountant lost over Rs 21 crore in six months after fraudsters posing as investment advisers lured him via WhatsApp

title: Amid Restrictions, J&K Minister Tries To Visit Martyrs' Graveyard In Burqa
content: All roads leading to the martyrs graveyard in downtown Srinagar have been sealed amid a massive security deployment in the city.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei Ready To Face Public? His Father's Memorial May Hold Answers
content: The 56-year-old cleric has been noticeably absent during the funeral prayers for his father last week, which were led by three of his brothers.

title: Will Fuel, LPG Prices Rise Again? Experts Flag Risks As Iran War Escalates
content: While crude oil supplies appear relatively better insulated, industries dependent on LPG remain vulnerable if disruptions worsen.

title: Karnataka BJP MLA Attends Criminal's Birthday Bash, Sparks Controversy
content: The grand birthday celebration was held in Alur in the Yelahanka constituency, where the criminal, known as King Maker Das, was seen celebrating with BJP MLA SR Vishwanath and several BJP leaders.

title: Mapping The Odyssey: The Geography Behind Homer's Epic Journey
content: The ancient Greek historian Polybius, who came 600 years after Homer, believed The Odyssey was a real story with some myths, rather than the reverse.
